meaning: adj.怪诞的,奇异的,不安的,可怕的
examples: The unique appearance may be eery when we first see it.
Mitochondria are tiny energy units within eery cell of the body.
There should be a better way to start a day than waking up eery morning.
In fact, as we shall see, it is almost eery how currently applicable entire sections of the prose are.
This game is film noir style, simple, yet eery black and white.
